title: Pentacalia libertatis
taxonomy: Pentacalia libertatis is a species in the plant family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Pentacalia, which comprises approximately 220 species of flowering plants. Pentacalia libertatis is native to Central and South America, where it grows in a variety of habitats, including forests and mountainous regions. The classification of Pentacalia libertatis is further divided into its kingdom, as a plant, it belongs to the kingdom Plantae. Further classification includes its phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species, which are all part of the binomial nomenclature system.

intro: Pentacalia libertatis, also known as Libertad's pentacalia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia libertatis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pentacalia libertatis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, colds, and other respiratory issues.
